events.artist: Knocking on the Door of Tomorrow
events.description: Before the festival fully wakes, we knock. A small greeting to the land that's about to hold us, to let the air rearrange itself around us. The door is already open, we are guests here.
A landing, of sorts, though arrival is always a bit of a fiction. We come in carrying what we're carrying, and we put some of it down. Integration before, during, and after. Welcome home.
Bow to the wow, bow to the brainbox, bow to the body, bow to whatever brought you here. The wow bows back. The wow is about to unfold. So begins the bowing.
No rope, no restraint, yet somehow you're held. Guided imagery leads the body through tension, surrender, and the delicious edge of the unseen. Bound by nothing, held by imagination.
You've never played bingo like this. A night full of drag, performances, sass, and punk, hosted by our iconic Venga Girls. Come for the game, stay for the drama, and win sexy prizes!
The cosmic joke slowly reveals itself: you are a sentient piece of stardust trying to remember you are actually the entire galaxy. The universe laughs at itself through you.
Touch is a language older than words. Here, we remember how to speak it.
Each of us is inside our own little universe. Somehow, this becomes a collective. We arrive as ourselves and begin there alone, together.
Your body is a galaxy of restless atoms pretending to be still. Shake, swirl, and drift through movement where imagination meets physics. The groove was always microscopic.
A hundred bodies, one strange creature. We start as cells and end as something with paws and instincts. Come as you are and become something else.
Carmen Villain is the recording project of Carmen Hillestad, a Norwegian-Mexican artist known for hazy ambient, dubwise electronics, woodwinds and spacious experimental composition.
Afro-cubano chekeres amplifying the sounds of Cuban resilience. The jing: a deep Korean gong swallowing silence like an egg eating snake.

Producer, DJ and composer born in suburban Detroit, now based in London (via Mexico City). Co-founder of the Human Pitch label with Simisea and Lot Radio resident; weaves modular synthesis, cello, hand-drumming and spoken word across club and ambient iterations (Wisdom Teeth, 3024, Banoffee Pies).
His fingers don't play the tombak and daf, they whisper to them, argue with them, bring to life unmapped cartographies. A metronome that dreams.
Lisbon DJ active in the local scene with appearances at Arroz Estúdios, Carpintarias de São Lázaro and the ELA collective's Earth Night events.
Syncopated drums, marimba lines, warm synths. Dance music with pools of personality, swagger and soul.

A growing table, rotating DIY workshops, weird materials, and good company. Weaving, animating, sewing, synthesising, tarot-making, incense-rolling and more. Find a seat, something's always happening.

CALMA. A durational performance where a sculptural element is slowly transformed into a living wind chime. Through gentle movement, sound and light, the space is subtly activated. What emerges is a fleeting, poetic moment, and a resonance that remains beyond the night.

Sunburn Gaze. A lost urban artefact. A stage for the inspired. A space for social encounters and strange visions.. What happens in the bus stop, no one talks about. But the lights keep flickering as if something weird will happen tonight.
minipops. A side-quest where embracing old technology and forgotten traditions will surface questions that only your elders have an answer to.

Mopets off-grid. CALMA. If you are lucky you will find CALMA in the deep wilderness of the terrain.. a hidden space to rest your spirit & lounge in nature at daytime and catch performative surprises & light art at night...

We are the stone house, you are the milk, drawn from the silence of granite and silk. A psychedelic picnic for the soul unfolds in the cottage no one can control. Casa Marmelada is open.. then not, a door that remembers what footsteps forgot. We lure you with stories, then send you along, you'll leave with a new tune, a different song. No beans will we spill, not a secret, nor clue just sneaky-peek knowing between us and you. So come to the edge of the nearly-unseen: the stone house is pouring from places between.

Parker Heyl. Two kinetic Chandeliers inspired by the 1875 work of mathematician Harry Hart. They echo the complexity of digital imagery, yet these sculptures achieve a physical object aura by using purely analog and mechanical technologies.
A living apple tree suspended in a white dreamworld. No gatekeepers, no agenda. Come lie down and remember you're made of mud.

Service Continu. A sensory refuge, this 'cold sauna' extends the lake's cooling effect through a simple closed-loop water system. Lake water is pumped to create a gentle artificial rain, cooling the space. Filtered light, dampened sound, and reclaimed materials create a low-tech, reversible retreat for rest and reset.
Sew, repair, upcycle... Drop off your textile leftovers while you're at it. Repair is radical, and honestly quite satisfying.
Free period care, herbal tea, a cool breeze, and zero shame. Also: a parade. You'll know it when you see it.
Old PVC banners become handmade pouches and bags, one-of-a-kind and built for dancing. Your hands do the work, your imagination does the rest.
Listen to your body, then sew it a little, friend. Take it, leave it, or give it away... you decide where your cushion goes pushin'.

c.a.l.perspectives. Installations of inhabitable camera obscura, offering direct contact with the natural phenomenon that makes photography possible. Enter into relationship with the upside down, and an archaeological experience of the image.
Daily sessions, live models, themed nights. Full permission to look intensely at another human being.
Live, improvised, unplanned. Music that emerges from the day and disappears into the night. Curious ears and spontaneous collaborators welcome!
Banned films. Fake candles. Strange conversations. Find it if you can.
Brittany Maton, Eleni Giannopoulou & others. A floating masquerade without a schedule. Part ritual, part hallucination, part boat. Somewhere between folklore, science-fiction, and the weird.
A haircut as a conscious ritual. Trust the hands, surrender the hair, leave somehow different.

Guardians of Words. A curated sanctuary of textual spirituality and poetry with spirit. Join the Guardians of Words to explore the cosmos by peeking into new perspectives through the bewildering spell of language.
Write what you're grateful for, bless some sad-looking plants, bring them to the lake, wash each other's hands, make incense to take home. Mona holds the space. The rest is magic.
Make a tiny animation, add it to everyone else's, and receive the collective film at the end. In Farsi, ham-nafas means sharing the same breath, so inhale.
Create a self-portrait on a tile from foraged materials. Hang it on the wall, and become part of a collective class picture.

Paint with everything you've ever lived through, in coordination with others. Beginners, advanced, and the curious are all welcome in this open fruit-salad activity. Just try it out, or come and watch.
JeJoMaDe. A platform by the waterfront where people can chill, wonder and be pleasantly confused. A confusing mix of harbour & cheese, many options to hang & sit, swing, nibble & crawl.

Zora Snake and Wilfried Nakeu. A burial and liberation ritual. L'Opéra du Villageois denounces the European museums that harbour looted art in their collections and conjures up the spirit of the stolen objects. Snake reclaims everything that the colonial powers have plundered over the past centuries: the gold, the salt, the history. But their spirits are not dead...

Asquito Eso. A body both fragile and fierce, shapeshifting and endlessly reborn. A striptease unfolds not to seduce but to speak out. Here sensuality trembles with rage and anger undresses itself slowly. This body, improbably, beautiful, grotesque, defiant, wears its rawness like myth.
A 4x4m loom, lots of weird materials, zero instructions. Come weave something into existence.

Cella Collective. These lakeside structures turn local ecology, geology and folklore, into spaces for contemplative bathing and soaking. These dwellings house the 'Enchanted Moura' and they invite you to enjoy the threshold between land and water, waiting and becoming.

Vlenser. Scent slips past logic, straight into memory, into feeling - at Mimo, we follow this invisible thread. Locally gathered, constantly shifting, smell is our guide. No labels, no hierarchy - but sensation rewiring perception, dissolving limits, and pulling you deep into the olfactory subconscious.

events.artist: Miranda & the Andacondas present 'Judge not lest ye be judged'
events.description: Knetterbar. Knetterbar is a condensed microcarnival - a portal of kaos & magic, where time bends and joyfulness grooves through surreal scenes. Hosted by Miranda from the Multiverse and her highly professional this-year-not-so-randomly assigned assistants. Expect hijinks, volcanic dances, disco sound massages and mystery snacks. Reality? Maybe. Fun? Definitely.
Moon Shack, baby, moon shack! Confess to the moon, get a wish, and pass it around. She's listening.

Echo Studios. Mudança is inspired by butterfly evolution, structures across water reflect each phase. Visitors move through a living narrative. Strangers breathe together, less alone in change. Something shifts and loosens. We leave differently than we arrived, gently rearranged, not new, not whole.
Mess around on synths, record it on a cassette, keep the tape. Like a sound photograph, but cooler and slightly noisier.

Oliver Ellmers. Reimagining OvO as a darker, more minimal parametric sculpture. The design is generative in approach, using light, reflection and refraction to continuously reshape the space. It's intended as a calm but uncanny place to reset and drift through a more cinematic mood.
Whisper something into a plant. The rest is hard to explain.
Chess, but social. Music, fun, and a few surprises from a club that's taken over Lisbon, London, Oslo, and now Crato.

Robin Jae. Here, blind violence is rewarded, for a piñata is completed by its destruction! These days the game is performed for thrills and rewards. Yet, if the piñata is made to be a demon, or a spirit, its destruction becomes a symbolic gesture. O' brave warrior, your prize awaits... Come! Conquer! Liberate spirit!
A womb-like pod abandoned in the undergrowth by persons or civilisations unknown. Scientists are taking notes. You should probably go inside.
Azuru. Shhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhhh.

M.O.S.S.S. (Moving Observations on Surviving Soft Skills) & Landcare Club. Enter the long now, armed with affective tools and tear into small breaches in reality. With cryptogams as our kin, bryophytes, lichens, seaweed, fungi, ferns, we play with the invisible and kneel before mystery.

nitamortei + maud gyssels. Which human, inhuman, past and future voices can be heard in interaction with the lake as a source of histories, narratives and rituals? Its movement is circular. Consider it a looping song, an invitation to wade in the shallow waters.
Make your own tarot cards. Intuition required, art skills not so much.
A one-to-one therapeutic tarot reading in a softly held space. Not to predict your future, but to help you understand your present. Also roaming the festival for a one-card hit if you feel called.
A witches' kitchen where the plants finally tell you their secrets. Come after dark too, some of them glow.
Pétanque. Sun. Maybe pastis. Definitely new friends.

events.artist: Tuning folk, Tuning folks, Tuning forks
events.description: RÆV (Vera & Eva Maria). Equipped with tuning forks and other instruments, the Tuning Folk roam around, finding depleted souls, and nurturing them back to life.
Craft your own little container from bamboo and cork. For butts, treasures, or honestly whatever :)

Riley Davidson & Hadrien Daigneault-Roy. A response to the announcement from hydrologists of a global water bankruptcy, the performance treats water as precious alchemical matter, resonating at the core of our flesh. Quicksilver and tears, rivers and prayer.
Add your thread to a collective tapestry growing all week long. Weavever you go you are part of the pattern.
Improv, movement, character: step outside yourself for a bit. Unscripted, ungovernable.

ARAS, Coletivo Giria, Louise. A participatory crochet installation developed through workshops involving both residents of Crato and festival crew. Young and old and local and not, are invited to contribute with crochet 'granny squares' that will be assembled into a shared textile structure creating the intimate, cosy, colourful 'Crocave'.
Christian Wolf. Experience your body, experience embodiment in time and space, with your next one. Reflect, realize, create, be in the now.

'Music for Magic' ain't no metaphor, but a living technology for transformation. It's an insurgency against agreed-upon reality. Rhythms that rupture the mass hypnosis. Perceptual trapdoors swinging open.

DJ Marcelle / Another Nice Mess (Marcelle van Hoof): a fearless, playful pioneer who DJs and produces on three turntables at once, layering environmental sounds, heavy bass, post-punk, dub, weird hip hop and new African dance music. Releases on Klangbad (Faust) and Jahmoni.

Polish-born, Detroit-raised DJ/producer who rose through Richie Hawtin's M_nus camp; runs Perm Records. Precise, playful minimal techno, with projects reaching into art and tech. | Detroit-bred cult DJ now based in New York; longtime resident of The Bunker NY and a master of raw, sweaty acid-house sets.
Brass voltage. Mutant Electronics. Deep soundsystem pressure. Steam. Served Kampala hot. Tastes like nothing you've ever heard.

Anushka Manchanda: artist, producer and activist based between Mumbai and Barcelona, with an artistic legacy from Bollywood playback singing to film. Genre-bending live electronic sets weaving roots, growling bass, lush soundscapes and socio-political storytelling focused on nature and the rise of the feminine.

Rotterdam-rooted, Amsterdam-associated DJ celebrated for fearless, fast-moving genre-resistant sets that flip between broken beats, jungle, trance and oddities; De School alumna and festival favorite.
Between breath and movement there's a whole universe we rush past. Asana becomes less about arriving and more about what falls between the cracks on the way there.

events.artist: Fluid Stretch & Kardio with a K
events.description: The festivities never stop, nor does our movement, so let's ride it & flow among stretching poses, slowing down into the crucial spots that help us sur-rave-ive the week. Then we aerobic with it for a deep, manic sweat. #ThisIsNotYoga

A soft no to grind culture. We gather to undo and unravel, to slow down collectively, to remember rest as necessity not luxury, as the kind of refusal that builds the world we actually want to live in.

Gilles Aiken, Berlin DJ and producer making shadowy club tracks with a surrealistic, cosmic edge - house and techno echoing Brian Eno, Robert Fripp and Conny Plank (whom he has remixed). A mainstay of Berlin's WHITE label and Weimar's Giegling crew; latest album 'Into A Better Future'.
Two men. A 400-year-old Portuguese farm where revolution was once plotted. Out came not gunpowder but a saxophone stitched into a tapestry both delicate & intense.
Jim Coles' Om Unit project performing material from Acid Dub Studies: weighty dubwise electronics that connect classic soundsystem pressure with jungle, techno and contemporary bass music.
We've buried spontaneity under curated punchlines. A workshop in un-polishing, cancel performativity culture, embrace the stumble, the crack and the glorious fail.
Breath is a small daily miracle. Rhythmic breathing and movement stir the inner fire and send something electric through the system. Turns out simply being alive is pretty electric.

events.artist: A Dark Room with a View
events.description: Tired of the same old pornographic tropes of sex dungeons and hook-up spots? Curious to curate a different kind of erotic space? Then schnap into our darkroom discourse circle and whet your appetite.
DJ from the Kansai scene known for deep, unpredictable vinyl sets; a favorite in the international minimal circuit.
Somewhere between dance and jiu-jitsu lies a playful entanglement. A squishy dance fight club, kinda. A soft fight where nobody loses and everybody wins.
Detroit producer, percussionist and DJ connected to the city's early house and techno history, known for warm, soulful, rhythmically rich deep house.

Alias of Vincent Vidal, a soundsystem ambassador and low-frequency architect moving between spatial audio, PA engineering and live mixing. Founder of the Subwise sessions and the Headroom soundsystem in Lisbon; affiliated with Studio DB Berlin.

events.artist: Charlemagne Palestine, Oren Ambarchi & Daniel O'Sullivan
events.description: One of the founders of the NYC school of minimalism music reimagines his 1997 meditative performance Karenina. Colourful consonance swelling until you can't tell where the room ends and your bloodstream begins.
Every body is fluent in something. Most of us were never taught how to read it. Through movement and sound, we expand the vocabulary and let our private languages meet on the floor.
Sara

A master of musical finesse: mischievous, seductive selections at Panorama Bar, Houghton and Waking Life, and deeper, more obscure experimental sets at Berlin Atonal and Draaimolen. Sleek yet raw, dark yet shimmering; also curates the SpeLL and dreamslesssweet projects. Russian-born, Berlin-based.
UK Garage was born to entertain soldiers. We trace the militarist-club nexus through Ayia Napa's haunted Cypriot psychogeography. A live improvised reckoning with bass and empire.
Psychedelic, free-form, high energy, spiritual dub-dance music.

Berlin-based DJ and Morphine Records & Ominira affiliate with impeccable, daring taste. Her psychedelic, no-boundaries sets have reached Terraforma, Intonal, CTM and Cafe Oto, and she hosts shows on Rinse France and Refuge Worldwide.
Tango is just leaning, with style. Into gravity, into rhythm, into another human. Two left feet welcome.
Rezmorah
Berlin-born vinyl-only DJ who honed her knowledge working at Hard Wax; rooted in American house and techno, with gigs at Blitz, Giegling parties and beyond.
Seattle-raised, Berlin-based DJ known for mind-bending, genre-fluid bass-led sets that move between breaks, dub and trippy club music.
Sonja & Ale
Even in stillness, the body hums. Explore the subtle landscape beneath the skin until the body feels less like an object and more like a galaxy.
Manuela · *Femmes Tipi
The great tree watches thresholds. Yoruba time-sorcery meets the dissident-transhuman body. A ritual of poetic contagion, you will most definitely not stay the same shape afterward.

A quiet inauguration; revealing, unveiling. We invite weary and curious bodies into our amphitheater of denim and dust. Let the first images of the weekend wash over you. Some things are better discovered than described. Schnap into it!
Pairing of German underground artists XDB and Lowtec, both known for patient, dubby, deeply textured house and techno rooted in small-label dance music culture.
Flamenco's beautiful heretic. For twenty years he's been unpicking flamenco's seams, rewiring its soul.
London artist and DJ formerly associated with Sicaria Sound; her solo work as Mia Koden focuses on dark, percussive UK bass pressure, dubstep weight and grime-informed club music.

Nuno Costa, Lisbon DJ and producer; co-founder of the Hayes Collective. Syncopated, Detroit-leaning techno with releases on Synewave NY, Faut Section and Hayes; a regular at Lux Frágil, Gare and Ministerium.

Drawing on archival and everyday footage – often shaped by a colonial gaze – three films reclaim and reimagine perceptions of Palestine through experimental image-making that blurs documentary and fiction.  UNDR by Kamal Al Jafaari The flowers stand silently witnessing by Theo Panagopoulos Home Movies Gaza by Basma Al Shari

Witches Were Right. A collective conjuring of the unseen forces we possess. A sonic bed invoking the powers and wisdom of the roots running across the veil of the seen and unseen. Vibrations that resonate from the depths and live in all our bones.

A camera tethered to a 100-meter cord descends into a single well – continuously, spiraling, without arrival. Inspired by early phantom ride films of the late 19th century, this work redirects cinema's oldest illusion of motion downward into geological and psychic depths. Stone becomes echo. Engineering becomes oracle.
Manchester producer associated with improvised, hardware-shaped house and techno, often released anonymously or with little biographical framing.

A Berlin-based collective of filmmakers and cinephiles dedicated to independent SWANA (Southwest Asia & North Africa) cinema. Through pop-up screenings worldwide, they scout raw talents and untold stories – bridging cultures, preserving heritage and amplifying voices that deserve a bigger screen.  White Winged Horse by Mahyar Mandegar Sultana's Reign by Hadi Moussally Ship of Fools by Alia Haju

DJ Scotch Egg meets Raja Kirik in a collision named after the sound of a wooden fish being struck. Industrial. Joyful. Completely unclassifiable. The name says it all and nothing, you'll have to feel it for yourself.

Founding member of Spiral Tribe and R-Zac, co-founder of Network 23 Recordings and a key innovator of the 90s Hardtec/Tribe scenes. Pushing live-improvised hard techno since 1991; recent project 'Wave Arising' blends ambient, tribal, experimental and hard techno, named one of the standout performances at Dekmantel 2024 by Resident Advisor.
Pioneer Mark Lawrence from South Norwood, London; half of Digital Mystikz and co-founder of DMZ and Deep Medi Musik. Played a defining role in dubstep before it even had a name.
w/ Moonscreen — EFF Barcelona

events.artist: Tiny Man Festival: Experimental Animated Shots
events.description: Tiny Man, the interplanetary creature of extraordinary dimensions, returns to Earth for another round of terrestrial displays of stories being told. A yearly challenge, we ask for creatives of all shapes to tell their best tales based on a theme. Short and animated, this is an anthology of trips, Trips and TRIPS from around the world, done exclusively for the Tiny Man.

Three black mountains blink down on aeons of human doing and undoing, while a geologist and an indigenous poet search the Amazonian landscape for clues from which to reconstruct a fast vanishing past.

DJ who came up through Rio de Janeiro's scene and is now based in Barcelona, with French-Brazilian roots. Energetic vinyl sets weaving house, acid and trance, informed by a drummer's sense of groove; founded the Pulso label.
Tokyo-based DJ, resident of the legendary Future Terror parties alongside DJ Nobu and a leading figure in Japan's dance music scene; deep, spiraling techno sets.
Producer Ruben Üvez, of Turkish heritage, crafting spacious, hypnotic, polyrhythmic tracks on Nous'klaer Audio and Spazio Disponibile.

Habits run deep, deeper than we want to admit, into body and nervous system. We loosen the loop with play, pause, and movement we don't quite recognise. The false idea of control gets a friendly poke.
Category is: dance or die. Face, European Runway, Old Way Vogue, Femme. Ballroom as queer survival technology. Learn moves, grab attention, thrive in self-love and the warm, sassy hug of community.

Glossy Mario is a Madrid-based selector and founder of Glossy Mistakes, known for a borderless, deep-digging approach to obscure records, reissues and atmospheric music. Moving between leftfield sounds, forgotten gems and unexpected sonic connections, his selections reflect a wide-ranging curatorial style also heard through appearances on platforms like Kiosk Radio's Outsiders series.
Movement and stillness, light and shadow, the loud and the quiet. The body holds all of it, and we use breath, mantra, and movement to tune to whichever one we've been avoiding.
Selectora and radio host from Monterrey, based in Amsterdam. Hosts 'Club Coco' on NTS, specialising in Brazilian, Latin American and Caribbean wax.
An exploratory improvised live set featuring bespoke instruments made here by the lake.
The body as sculpture, except it breathes, leaks, and changes its mind. Move through strange shapes, states, and impulses, and let the body show you what it makes when you stop telling it what to do.
London-based multi-disciplinary artist, record collector and DJ; manager of the Hidden Sounds record store and NTS resident, playing deep, spiritual and psychedelic selections.
Smash. Crush. Eat. Ruin. Transform. A performance-workshop where objects become sacrilege, and composting becomes kink. What lives at the edge of the ruins? You.

The sky is not a backdrop, it's a stage and right now the performance is wild. Saturn dismantling, Neptune dissolving, karmic axis shifting, world breaking and blooming at the same time. We read the drama above to understand the trembling below.
Drums that listen. Guitar that shapeshifts into vapour. Hazy, nocturnal, wandering like a half-remembered dream.
Felipe
Long-running project of George Evelyn from Leeds, a Warp Records mainstay known for smoky downtempo, sample-rich trip hop, soul, dub and mellow dance music.
Co-founder of [a:rpia:r] with Raresh and Petre Inspirescu; a foundational figure of the Romanian minimal sound and one of its most revered selectors.
The body as tide, since the sonic wave is improvised and the room is never twice the same. Qi Gong meets live sound, and the body learns to move at the speed of breath.

Harness the pulse. Become the bass. Become the gospel. (The rest is a secret the grave won't keep.) The commodification of the rave will not stand. Group thoughts on the dancefloor industrial complex.
Carmo
Born in jaipong clubs at 2am, cigarettes dying in ashtrays, someone muttering about ghosts. A friendship stretched across twelve time zones. The distance itself became the music.
Nicolas Chaix records as I:Cube, a long-running French producer on Versatile Records whose work spans deep house, electro, disco mutations and cinematic electronics.

A social tango space for the curious, the shy, and the rhythmically adventurous. Learn the quiet etiquette of the ronda, the cabeceo's silent invitation, and let yourself drift into the orbit of the dance.
German producer and DJ, one half of Session Victim, known for warm, sample-laced house music, deep grooves and live-leaning club productions.
Sabrina · *Femmes Tipi
Em
Brussels-based DJ (aka OJOO GYAL), selector, organiser and community builder of Moroccan origin, channeling North African rhythms into genre-defying, bass-conscious club sets.
When poetry meets music in a smokey bar; melancholic, dramatic, and strangely comforting at the same time.
A gremlin-nymph rises through frequencies and mist. Not human, not myth, just pure, delightful weird. Descend with us, shed some heaviness, resurface a little more tender and porous.
Lie down and let the unseen do the talking. Gongs, chimes, and pauses ripple through you while your body remembers how to rest. Nothing to do but listen while the invisible rearranges you.

events.artist: A Homage to Tiny Companions with Diaskoop
events.description: These small compositions were made earlier at the Landcare Club. Join us for an appreciation of seed fluffs & flower petals to discover more about the land, or just because they're beautiful!

K is a solo that explores the body as a field of forces in transformation. In dialogue with space and environment, sound, light, and movement intertwine to create an intense and immersive WOW. A performance that e x p a n d s perception.
Detroit-rooted producer, DJ and Aesthetic Audio founder whose music is known for raw, soulful deep house and techno with a strong underground sensibility.

A DEBAUCHEROUS SPELL. Progressive music, effective words, metaphysical realness. Audience invited to move choreographically. sstillnnessabused. The frequency of EVERYthing. The elemental PEOPLECY ++++humOUR essential.
Detroit legend Brian Jeffries, pioneering ghettotech specialist. High-octane turntablism fusing electro, Miami bass and booty house; runs Databass Records.
Lisbon DJ and producer Tiago Miranda, a cornerstone of the local scene (Lux Frágil) with releases on DFA and Ene Records.
The sequel. If you were there last year, you know what madness to expect. If you weren't... well.. you kinda had to have been there.. ;) FYI, this is also your moment to shine. Yeah you :)
Leeds-based turntablist, composer and sound artist; first Black turntablist to win an Oram Award. Immersive, sample-rich composition and improvisation.

This evening offers two works by Iranian women – each finding their own language for displacement, memory and what the body carries across borders.  Unheld III explores tender moments of disassociation in female bodies – a requiem for displacement, holding fragments of home while embracing an identity that can only emerge at a distance. Through improvised yet carefully mapped performance, Kimia's wearable Alloy Resonator transforms her body into instrument & Saghi's intimate archive of haptic encounters with flora and female tenderness builds a world between dislocation and connection.

Hyojin Min, a rising DJ from Seoul (active since 2017, BBCB resident) now based in Germany and aligned with the Giegling label. Dreamy, gleaming sets criss-crossing psychedelic disco, minimal acid and meandering '90s techno, mostly below 125 BPM; made her production debut with the aqueous house/techno EP 'JIN 09'.

Maryam Tafakory layers poetry, archival fragments and speculative nonfiction into films that move through historical gaps and unspoken rules. Her practice traces quiet acts of erasure – upon bodies, intimacies, histories – giving space to concealed queer stories and what is too often dismissed as excessive or trivial.  Nazarbazi Razeh-Del Daria's Night Flower
Ziggy Devriendt from Ostend, founder of the Stroom label and radio platform; a digger with wide-ranging, eccentric taste in forgotten Belgian and global music.

A durational sonic séance of deep frequencies, strings, chants, herbal potions, and an altar for the departed. Mourning as collective resonance. Rest among ghosts. Dawn comes tender. Come lay with us.

events.artist: Curated by SUNA & Erotic Film Festival Barcelona
events.description: Erotic Film Festival Barcelona and Suna present a juicy selection of films that explore desire, bodies and sexuality with nuance: playful, political, intimate, challenging. The night then climaxes in a participatory performance streamed live to the big screen straight from across the road in Suna.

events.artist: The Rabbit and the Magic Man
events.description: bondage, joy, brute force
DJ, producer and vocalist raised between Vienna and the UK, with a long history at Panorama Bar and clubs worldwide; a respected deep house and techno voice for two decades.
Rafael Vogel records as Leafar Legov, a German artist associated with Giegling, known for intimate, melancholy ambient, house and minimal techno.
Vinyl-focused DJ who moves freely across styles, drawing inspiration from travel, ritual and collective experience; co-founder of the Tempio del Futuro Perduto project in Milan.

events.artist: A Quiet Place for Quiet People
events.description: A soft landing for the sensitive and the overstimulated. Move slowly, breathe deeply, and enjoy the relief of not having to say anything at all. Belonging without words.
In-tense. Out-tense. Sus-tense. Psychedelic breathwork to transcend, rewire, and return replenished. Breathe like you mean it.
A quiet, playful descent beneath thinking. The body remembers what it knew before language, the child still somewhere in there starts speaking again in sensation, image, and memory.
One guitar. A single thread tugging you through fado's exquisite grief, flamenco's gut-punch, bossa nova's tenderness. A poet who sings and a singer who poems. Lisbon's quiet earthquake.
Georgian DJ and producer connected to Tbilisi's underground club scene, with sets that move through techno, electro and sharp-edged experimental dance music.

Each layer deeper, each threshold asking you to set something down. Through astrology, trance, and sound, we move beneath the surface of the mind, through the seen forces and the unseen ones, and meet what's already waiting.
Pain into pleasure. Vulnerability, trust, consent. Permanent marks from a still-shaping angle. Tattoo as collective transformation.
Chicago-born DJ and producer, now based in Brussels. Sundaze resident; released the acclaimed 'Basic Moves 18' double EP and appears on Die Orakel. Rugged electro with wiggly basslines.
Leopoldo Rosa records as Lerosa, an Italian-born, Dublin-based producer known for melodic electro, acid, deep house and techno on labels such as Lunar Disko and Further.
Touch your friends. Touch like a river. Touch like a rock. Gift them fire, blow on them like wind. A collaborative touch-gift ecology opens.
A push and pull between chaos and calm, between sound and stillness, between what the body hears and what it answers with. Silence moves. The body listens. Something subtle begins to speak.
French producer and DJ associated with the late-1990s French touch sound; co-creator of Stardust's 'Music Sounds Better With You' and founder of Vulture Music.
Icon of Angolan music.
Goddess Eileen & Slave Rui
When intimacy meets currency, what do we learn about ourselves? No judgment. Just real talk from those who know. Come into the circle.
Before music, before language, there was vibration. The universe clears its throat and we prepare to listen.
Finnish electronic composer and producer known for the Colundi sequence, intricate microtonal or frequency-based melodic systems, and a vast catalogue of crystalline techno experiments.
Aria & Annalisa

Thirty years defining post-rock's skeleton. Tonight they shed skin. Rhythm-driven electronica and modular pieces so precise they make you want to dance and meditate simultaneously. Sit still. Move. Both at once.

Sensuality is intrinsic, not learned. Humour is a rebellion against perfectionism. Together they take the neural pathway less traveled, the wild one, the soft one, the one covered in paint. If you can laugh, your body probably feels safe enough to be here.
Miranda, Himawari, Sad.ropes…
Deep, hypnotic alias of Amsterdam techno veteran Steve Rachmad, explicitly listed among his aliases on RA; classic 90s Parallel 9 material was reissued on Delsin.
*Femmes Tipi
Achim Brandenburg, better known as Prosumer, is a German DJ and producer associated with Panorama Bar and a deeply musical approach to house, disco and techno.

events.artist: Roots in Resonance — South Asian Diaspora
events.description: A welcome ceremony. Decolonising yoga over childhood snacks. Tracing queer histories. Bharatnatyam & Kuchipudi dance performance. How our migrant backgrounds shape the way we show up in the world and tinge our artistic expressions in electronic music.
Founders of decade-old Lisbon label Paraíso and producers of the eponymous documentary. Their DJ set pays homage to the chillout rooms of 90s raves – downtempo, low BPM, unhurried..
Ever heard polyrhythmic afro-deep-house, kuduro and minimal povera jazz all in one place? Well Lisbon based DJ Nigga Fox, DJ Danifox and DJ Firmeza are gonna bring it.
Colombian artist and DJ whose sets and productions move between ambient, experimental electronics, slow club music and Latin American musical references.
Worship at the altar of summer in sweet sweet song. Hallelujah, but make it queer.

Gilles Aiken, Berlin DJ and producer making shadowy club tracks with a surrealistic, cosmic edge - house and techno echoing Brian Eno, Robert Fripp and Conny Plank (whom he has remixed). A mainstay of Berlin's WHITE label and Weimar's Giegling crew; latest album 'Into A Better Future'.

In the early 90s, Portugal ignited its own dance music explosion – wild raves birthing a sound uniquely its own. Through exclusive footage and interviews with DJs, producers, dancers and promoters, this documentary traces the rise of Portuguese rave culture and its lasting echo in electronic music worldwide.
We carry hidden rooms inside us, latent versions of ourselves waiting for someone to hold the key. Something forms in the space between. Not you, not me. Something shared.
The double bass hums at the edge of hearing. A slow-moving tide of sound. Lie down and float.
Munich-born DJ and producer of Turkish heritage, shaped by a decade in New York's ballroom scene. Blitz Club resident and Panorama Bar regular; debut EP 'Maktub' (2023).

Scenographer Luisa Lukas weaves digitised home videos from 1998–2004 into a live audiovisual meditation on the fragility of early childhood. Through datamoshing, pixel distortion and layering, images dissolve as memories do - into fragments and emotional traces. Sound and image unite, built intuitively, collapsing into silence. Each iteration is unique, specially reworked for Moonscreen.
Sam Shackleton is a British producer known for hypnotic, percussion-heavy bass music, from Skull Disco's dubstep-era mutations to later ritualistic techno and experimental live work.

Episodic memories are distilled into sense impressions. Where last year's Drift was pre-recorded, introspective, rising from a nighttime stillness into a din at dawn, Field is live, connected, beginning in the commotion of a daylight gathering & landing us softly back where we started: a quiet lake shore at night.
Tension. Limits. Release. Rope and resonance in the space-in-between. Witness what's possible when edges become portals and the skin the primary organ of sense-making.

events.artist: Joshua Serafin & Mario Barrantes Espinoza w/ Fake Moss
events.description: Fractured Divinities meets Relics meets Maxhete. Exactly.
DJ and promoter based in Graz, Austria; part of the Interlude crew and founder of ISKRA in Belgrade. Patient, immersive genre-free journeys.

Globally, data is kept captive. Here, unshackled, it flows between machine, coders, and audience. Human-typed code generates art. No AI. Livecoding jam: we show our screens; you interpret the creation process. Visual and sonic landscapes, ephemeral and improvised through custom software. Breaking loops and looping breaks – notbinarycode strikes again.

Confirmed as Tobias Freund: highly regarded producer, sound engineer and musician (aliases Pink Elln, Tobias.). Decades of experimental, precision-engineered electronic music; a master of analog gear and improvisational live performance.
Gyrofield is a Hong Kong-born producer pushing technoid drum & bass and exploratory club pressure; Upsammy (Thessa Torsing) is known for crystalline, fluid IDM and broken techno.
New York-based DJ and NTS resident known for agile, atmospheric sets that connect techno, breaks, electro and leftfield club music.
DJ and producer; regular at Zurich's Supermarket Club, has played Closer (Kyiv) and Guesthouse (Bucharest), collaborates with Giegling and runs the Interlude label.
SnPLO pairing: PLO Man (Berlin-based) with fellow countryman and longtime collaborator DJ Spence, focused on deep, dense and exploratory dub-techno dancefloor music.
As the sun slowly rises, a story begins to unfold. Voices, rhythm, and imagination drift between dream and daylight. The morning is listening.

Don't kill intelligence, just land it in the body. Low frequencies, subtle shifts, deep listening. Sound moves through you tuning the system from the inside. Less about hearing. More about feeling what's already there.
German electronic musician whose work as Jan Jelinek, Farben and Gramm helped define a warm, crackling strand of minimal electronics, sample-based ambient and dub-techno abstraction.
For mornings when the body is heavy and the soul is slightly off-axis. Slowwww movement, breath, and bodywork to oil the joints and put the pieces back together again.
Two members of Chicago's The Sea and Cake: Sam Prekop's melodic modular language with John McEntire's compositional and production craft (duo album 'Sons Of' on Thrill Jockey).
Siblings serving luau guitar-ska-rockabilly-R&B chaos.

events.artist: Performing with Pride ft. Brian the non-binary Lion
events.description: A musical theatre werkshop focusing on queer expression, character embodiment and finding power in voice. Part storytelling, part vocal technique and full on musical-theatre-showtime.. roar :)
Yin postures slow the body down. Live double bass meets it there, somewhere below language. Surrender is the only verb available.
Butch Morris gesture based conduction tradition. Enter sonic improv with your full cellves. No net. No fear. Just game.
Milan-based producer, DJ, sound artist and independent researcher in sound cultures, exploring murky bass, broken rhythms and industrial-leaning club music (Ilian Tape, Mille Plateaux).
DJ and producer now based in Berlin; regular at Club der Visionäre, The Pickle Factory and Waking Life. Vibrant, hazy house with the Risky Bookings crew.
Southern Moroccan desert. Gnawa as living memory. Stories of slavery, migration, spiritual endurance carried in a woman's voice that could crack stone. Desert blues meets trance-state invocation.

A late-festival session for late-festival bodies. Mantra, movement, contact, and a bit of soul pondering that takes you the rest of the way to the wellspring inside you that never actually had walls to begin with.
DJ, producer, promoter and driving force behind the Elberec label; Disturbia Amsterdam resident. Originally from Portugal, now based in the Netherlands.
Argentinian producer and DJ associated with detailed, rolling techno and minimal forms, with releases on labels such as Ilian Tape and Greener.
Gigi & Mari
Strangers become waves. Touch = gospel. Sound = glue. A sonic love cult. Dive in or drift away. The flow is giving aliveness. Not a performance but a melthood, ooze in?
All's good my heart. The longest day deserves a chorus. Somewhere between devotion and chaos, we sing.
Lisbon duo of DIY acid-techno producers and live performers (Acid Mary aka Rubina and Jack Drop aka André), celebrating the analog sound with spontaneous, improvised hardware sets.
Arrived in Lisbon at 18 with Santiago Island lodged in his marrow. Turned longing luminous. Synthesizers bent to Cape Verdean soul. Reggae and funaná tangled into something no shoreline can contain.
Rodrigo
German producer known for playful, melodic minimal house and microhouse with a dubby, late-night touch.
Roots and dub soundsystem session; believed to be the Lisbon-based Simply Rockers hi-fi crew (unconfirmed - verify with the festival).
Where sonic memories from a distant past reverberate in dreams of an obscure postcapitalist future drowned in sedatives.
Carrier is the dub-influenced bass project of producer Guy Brewer; Gavsborg is co-founder of the Equiknoxx crew, known for off-kilter experimental dancehall.
Jairo & Julia
Thomas Melchior's microhouse/minimal vocabulary (Perlon, Playhouse) back-to-back with Hamburg Dial co-founder Lawrence's deep, melodic house-and-techno language: two veterans in dialogue.
Roots/dub collaboration session; Vedic Roots is believed to be a Portuguese roots reggae outfit joining the Simply Rockers soundsystem (unconfirmed - verify with the festival).
Didi & Teresa · *Femmes Tipi
Ghanaian vocal acrobatics. Syllable-bender. Smile-generator since '94. Discovered on Awesome Tapes from Africa. Sounds like surfing on pure sunshine.
Swedish DJ, producer and label figure associated with Kontra-Musik, known for deep, exploratory selections across techno, house and experimental club music.
Artist based between Berlin and Paris, running Fullpanda Records. Moves between stripped-down techno and poetic ambient/AV performances (Raster-Noton releases).

An interactive telepathic performance where the boundaries between minds dissolve. Through sound, ritual, and immersive magic, a portal to the invisible realm opens. Dark secrets are revealed. Unspoken questions find their answers. Personal messages are channeled from the unseen.

Born on the slopes of Etna, untethered from any geography. Nomadica curates screenings, events and workshops at the crossroads of experimental arts. They explore the uncertain relationship between cinema's optical eye and the ever-changing natural world – a journey between forest floor and incandescent sky.  Discoveries on the Forest Floor by Charlotte Pryce LANDFILL 16 by Jennifer Reeves Through the Lens by Cécile Fontaine Under the Volcano by Colectivo Los Ingrávidos The Astronomer's Dream by Julie Murray Luminous Variations in the City Skies by Giuseppe Spina Sun Song by Joseph Bernard

As our journey nears its end, drop into these dreamlike short films. Luminous works that create a gentle space for processing the week's ride.  Subtle Matter Fluid Hands by Maika Garnica Joon by Marian Aliabadi Earth Body I by Arran Gregory Top View of My Brain as a Crime Scene by Luzia Johow The Cast by Leon Billerbeck Gáthra by Camilla Dubhe and White Houston A shell of (for) you by Julia Chetwood UM)bigo by Sofia Santa-Rita Sunset Special by Nicolas Gebbe Baile do Sol by Miguel Tavares
Poetry with a live band. A sensory reckoning. No polish. No filter. Just beautiful, bleeding raw life.
Madrid-based producer and DJ with a vast hardware-driven catalogue ranging from lush soundscapes to deep, hypnotic techno (Cadenza, Analog Solutions).
Roots and dub soundsystem session; believed to be the Lisbon-based Simply Rockers hi-fi crew (unconfirmed - verify with the festival).

Sound unfolds as pure material: vibration, decay, interference. Visually, a drifting ecosystem of particles, spores and abstracted organic forms. Image and sound remain in deliberate tension, sustaining an atmosphere that refuses to resolve.
London DJ with 30+ years behind the decks, beloved for long-form, subtly psychedelic all-vinyl journeys. Co-runs Night Moves and Dream Bags parties.
French techno project known for immersive, cinematic live performances that pair austere deep techno with ambient interludes and spoken-word science-fiction atmospheres.

events.artist: Morada Aberta: Onde o Gesto Cura
events.description: Three interconnected moments, unfolding across languages, spaces and temporalities. Drawing from personal memory and the landscapes of North Portugal, Alto Minho and Galiza, this expanded cinema work follows working women - guardians of ancestral knowledge tied to land, sea and natural cycles. Between documentary and fiction, it traces the invisible gestures that sustain communities and keep sacred knowledge alive.
DJ/producer championed by Ben Klock; groove-centric, looping techno fusing detail and long-form storytelling, on Mote-Evolver and his own Moment label.

An endless back-to-back weaving four singular voices into one unbroken journey. Vera Heindel is a Frankfurt-scene selector with flawless technique and an ego-free, story-first approach, long associated with labels like Freebase and Half Baked. DJ Dustin is a founding force of the Weimar-based Giegling collective, known for deep, emotional house and techno that drifts between melancholy and release. M I M I (Maria Green) is a Brussels-based artist and DJ who has lived in Belgium since 1998, exploring her Afro-European cultural heritage through sound and various media. Amsterdam's Guido Janssen — Vuur — is an IsBurning resident and Bar Pamela programmer whose sexy, groove-driven house sets made their Boiler Room debut at DGTL 2023. Together they thread minimal, deep house and groove-led techno into a single, ever-unfolding narrative.
Berlin-based producer/DJ making playful, percussive and exploratory club music drawing on musique concrète, free jazz and Afrofuturist ideas (Don't Be Afraid, !K7).
Sound researcher. Deep thinker. Local fixture. Colours his worlds lush, ethereal, diffused. Soft edges to hold onto as the sun comes up.

Kia is the founder of the Animalia label and Cirrus, based in Melbourne, known for fluid, aquatic, psych-leaning selections; Richard Akingbehin is a British-Nigerian, Berlin-based DJ, label head and Refuge Worldwide co-founder with a dub-techno depth.
Ambient drone from astral vibrations.
Brussels DJ and producer chasing the 'trance dance experience': 90s tribal techno meets contemporary psychedelia. Kiosk Radio regular, involved in Horst and Stelplaats.
Multi-instrumentalist. Heartstring plucker. If this doesn't leave you tender, check your pulse.

Krautrock motorik flirts with dub gravity. Songs arrive like transmissions from the furthest wormhole, quantum, fantastical, but their real terrain is earthly: vulnerability, friction, the small mythologies of survival.
London producer and turntablist Felix Manuel, celebrated for emotional, genre-fluid productions and virtuosic three-deck vinyl sets. Releases on Houndstooth.
The revolution, jazzified. Diasporic-futuristic-transcultural big bang. Music. Spoken word. Movement. Prepare to tremble.
Brooklyn alchemist. Space as canvas. Modular synths as paint. Timbres so rich they require their own postal code. Longform pieces that build cathedrals in your cochlea.

events.artist: Colleen presents Libres Antes del Final
events.description: Bubble. Pulse. Ripple along with the frequencies of the Moog Matriarch. Go deep. Remember to surface for air. You might forget.
